subject: Gaming
title: Toxicity and Harassment Online
description: A micro-lesson that makes Toxicity and Harassment Online usable.

hook.text: A female developer received death threats for a game review. Online harassment is gaming's darkest problem.

buildup.text: Anonymity, competition, and passionate communities create conditions where harassment thrives.

discovery.text: Studies show women and minorities face disproportionately more abuse in online gaming spaces.

twist.text: Most platforms profit from engagement. Toxic interactions generate clicks. The business model resists reform.

climax.text: Solutions require redesigning systems, not just punishing individuals. The architecture enables the abuse.

punchline.text: Platforms profit from toxicity. Real change requires redesigning systems.

quiz.question: Why is online harassment difficult to solve in gaming?
options: Platforms profit from engagement, and toxic interactions generate clicks
